title: SAP tightens hiring, travel spend as it ramps up AI investments
description: German tech giant SAP is strategically curbing hiring, travel, and external spending to fuel its ambitious artificial intelligence investments. The company is prioritizing core AI roles and acquisitions, acknowledging rising costs associated with increased AI adoption. While non-essential internal travel is paused, customer-facing and AI-critical trips will proceed, reflecting SAP's commitment to its AI-driven future.
content:

title: NAREDCO Real Estate Conclave 2026 and NAREDCO NextGen NCR Conclave Conclude with Strong Consensus on AI and Sustainable Urban Development
description: New Delhi [India], July 4: The NAREDCO Real Estate Conclave 2026 and NAREDCO NextGen NCR Conclave, organised in association with Bharat Buildcon, held on 19 June 2026 at Yashobhoomi, New Delhi, concluded with a strong industry-wide consensus on accelerating the adoption of Artificial Intelligence, sustainable construction practices, integrated urban governance, and large-scale skill development to drive India's journey towards Viksit Bharat 2047.

title: The rise and rise of Nvidia and how it became world's most important company
description: It began with video games, a paintball experiment and a bold bet that few understood. Today, Nvidia has become a company every tech giant depends on to build the future of artificial intelligence.
content:
August 27, 2008. It's just after 3 pm in San Jose, California.
The large San Jose Convention Center is packed. Thousands of developers, gamers, engineers and technology enthusiasts have gathered for the grand finale of NVIDIA's three-day NVISION 08 c... [11651 chars]

title: AI must be built with Indigenous Knowledges, not against them
description: As Australia marks 50 years of NAIDOC Week, honoring the world's oldest living culture, humanity's newest technology has yet to reckon with a simple principle: "nothing about us, without us." The concern is that artificial intelligence (AI), like so many technologies before it, will become another extractive force that "takes" Indigenous Knowledges without consent, credit or return.

title: College of Charleston embraces AI in the classroom, teaching students to use it responsibly
description: While many colleges are banning generative artificial intelligence tools like ChatGPT, professors at the College of Charleston School of Business are taking a different approach: teaching students how to use them.
content:

title: Has Technology Ruined The World Cup?
description: This comes after FIFA invested years developing tools that combine player tracking, artificial intelligence and ball sensors to reduce human error in crucial moments.
content:
Soccer has always embraced innovation, albeit reluctantly.
In the 1990s, the back-pass rule changed the way teams built from the back. A decade ago, goal-line technology finally settled arguments over whether the ball had crossed the line.
